trend: Mild bearish for the next 1-3 trading days while NIFTY stays below 24343-24500; acceptance above 24500 would neutralize the bearish read, while loss of 24150/24086 confirms downside.
entry: Direction: sell rallies near 24280-24340 or sell breakdown below 24150; expected probability about 55%; risk-reward about 1:1.5 to target1 and 1:2.3 to target2.
stoploss: Above 24360 for tactical shorts; above 24500 on sustained acceptance for positional shorts
bullish_reasons: NSE index feed at 15:07 shows NIFTY 50 at 24218.9, +12.0 (+0.05%), still marginally above 24200 max pain after recovering from the 24000.2 intraday low.
NIFTY BANK is firmer at 58177.8, +131.9 (+0.23%), providing index support.
Latest available FII cash flow was +2603.7 Cr and DII cash flow was +2019.7 Cr as of 2026-07-10; current-day cash flow is not yet published during market hours.
Put-side positioning remains supportive: highest Put OI is at 24000, max pain is 24200, and change-OI PCR is high at 3.8293.
GIFT NIFTY is mildly positive at 24242.5, +0.17%, but below the +0.5% bullish scoring threshold.
bearish_reasons: FII index futures remain materially net short: 34427 long versus 289138 short, net short 254711 contracts.
India VIX is rising sharply near 13.27-13.29, about +8.4%, which is bearish under the scoring model.
PCR is elevated at 1.7075, above the model's 1.4 overheated threshold, so it is scored bearish despite put writing.
Fresh call writing is heavy at 24300, 24500, 24250, 24200, and 24400, capping upside near the current spot zone.
NIFTY remains below the 24343 expected-range upper band and the 24500 highest Call OI wall.
US cues are negative because Nasdaq futures are down about 0.91% and S&P 500 futures are down about 0.23%.
Asian cues are weak overall: Shanghai -2.06% and Nikkei -1.92%, with only Hang Seng slightly positive.
Rupee weakness near 95.72 per US dollar and Brent near $79.59 after a 4.7% jump are macro negatives for India.
Market breadth is soft: NIFTY 50 breadth 21 advances versus 29 declines; broad NSE breadth 4546 advances versus 4995 declines.
Option IV is not reliable in the accessible option-chain table because strike IV fields are zero; India VIX was used as the volatility proxy.
alerts: Strong Call Writing: heaviest fresh Call OI additions are at 24300, 24500, 24250, 24200, and 24400.
Strong Put Writing: change-OI PCR is 3.8293 with large Put additions at 24000, 24100, 24050, 24200, and 24150.
VIX Spike: India VIX is up about 8.4% near 13.27-13.29.
FII Reversal: FII cash is positive, but aggregate FII index futures remain heavily net short.
Breakdown Watch: loss of 24150/24086 would turn the recovery into downside continuation.
meaningful_changes: Fresh Strong Call Writing alert: heavy Call additions at 24300/24500/24250 now cap upside while the bias bucket remains MILD BEARISH.
Change-OI PCR increased from 3.4396 to 3.8293, strengthening put-side defence without changing the score bucket.
